T0158.title: Order Intake
T0158.definition: In illegal data trading, the act of a service provider receiving and accepting a client's data query order.
T0158.description: Within the underground data supply chain, 'accepting orders' is a signal from a service provider that they are open for business and can handle various requests such as personal background checks, transaction records, or location tracking. These order takers often act as middlemen who do not own the data sources themselves; instead, they aggregate orders and pass them to upstream interfaces or insiders, profiting from the markup.
